mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses forward earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points within a 3-month window
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and surge in nonfarm payrolls indicates a structural shift in consumer demand away from cyclical goods toward services, reducing industrial equity margins and pressuring forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint HOUST/PAYEMS signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a supply-constrained environment signals improved capacity utilization and pricing power for industrial equities, directly boosting forward earnings and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO prints do not correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the information coefficient drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales directly reduces near-term corporate revenue forecasts for consumer-facing sectors, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as analysts downgrade earnings.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 5 percent within a single reporting month
failure_condition: Retail sales drops do not lead to negative 20-day SPX returns in the subsequent quarter, or the signal generates a positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high policy rates and elevated inflation compress real yields and force prolonged monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuation multiples and drives negative forward returns over the quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL signal produces positive Rank IC over any 3-year window, or forward returns in high-rate/high-inflation regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands aggregate corporate earnings and improves business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the quarterly horizon as equity valuations reprice higher fundamental growth.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no predictive power for 60-day forward SPX returns, or the directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job creation alongside sub-4.5% unemployment signals a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and equity earnings, generating positive short-term returns as market participants price in sustained economic expansion.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS adds > 150000 and UNRATE remains below 4.0 percent
failure_condition: This labor market configuration fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS.
